description: Conversational food posts using dialect-heavy Arabic with rhetorical questions and casual tone to drive organic engagement through relatability and FOMO appeal.
structural_recipe: Use Saudi dialect (not formal Arabic) with rhetorical opening questions ('اللي ما جرّب...' / 'الي باقي ما...'). Include food product name prominently. Add casual emojis (😋, 🔥, 🧡) and promotion incentives ('وجبتك المفضلة', 'شوربة مجانية'). Close with branded hashtags (#مطاعم_الرومانسية). Keep 2-3 sentences max. Frame as peer-to-peer recommendation rather than brand announcement.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to casual peer recommendations over formal advertising. Rhetorical questions create psychological engagement ('وش تنتظر' / 'ما فاته شيء') by making followers feel they're missing out. Dialect-heavy content signals authenticity and trust. Food + incentive combos (free soup, special meals) trigger immediate call-to-action response.
transplantation_caveats: Requires native Saudi dialect fluency—poor dialect execution reads as inauthentic and reduces engagement
FOMO messaging may be perceived as aggressive in conservative contexts; tailor tone for brand voice
Rhetorical questions only work if product is genuinely popular; avoid for niche/new menu items
Free incentive mentions require clear T&Cs to avoid regulatory compliance issues in KSA
cultural_constraints: Use Saudi dialect exclusively; avoid Gulf-wide or pan-Arab phrasing
Food recommendations must align with local taste preferences (heavy on meat, broths, rice-based dishes)
Avoid gender-mixed imagery or non-halal product mentions
Promotion timing should avoid Ramadan fasting hours and prayer times for maximum reach
